project_description: Marketing site for Elitez Merchandising — Singapore's leading FMCG in-store merchandiser-promoter agency, a business unit of Elitez Group of Companies. Site presents the dedicated + shared merchandising service models, the proprietary EMR-Intel field-force management platform (mobile + QuickView + BI dashboards), tier-1 client outcomes (L'Oréal, Unilever, Shiseido, Pernod Ricard, Reckitt, FairPrice, Oatside, Suntory), and routes qualified FMCG brand and retail leads to Jack Wang (Associate Director).
constraints: B2B institutional tone — Elitez Group brand register (BCA / WSG / PDPC adjacent); no marketing-speak, no emojis in product copy

Do NOT publish specific client headcounts from the pitch deck (e.g. 'L'Oréal 14 pax', 'Unilever 60 pax'). Abstract to '350+ FMCG manpower island-wide' and 'managed teams of 5-60 pax per client' style ranges only.
Logo file canonical at /public/elitez-logo.png — never regenerate; never make Elitez logo dominant over the merchandising sub-brand wordmark
Footer attribution: 'Operated by Elitez Group of Companies' linking to https://elitez.com.sg
Eventual production domain: merchandising.elitez.com.sg (start on GitHub Pages, migrate later)
Singapore-only target geo for v1 (the merchandising business is SG island-wide; group-level SEA presence is brand context only)
Lead capture via Formspree → routes to jack.wang@elitez.asia (Associate Director, primary owner). Secondary CC to derrick@elitez.asia for executive visibility.
EMR-Intel platform is co-branded ('ELITEZ X EMR-Intel'); present as 'our field-force management system' — not as a product Elitez sells standalone

narrative_brief.what_the_business_does: FMCG in-store merchandising and promoter agency. Deploys, manages, trains, and supervises field merchandisers across Singapore supermarkets (FairPrice, Cold Storage, Giant, Sheng Siong), pharmacies (Watsons, Guardian, Unity), and other retail channels. Two service models: (1) Dedicated — full-time team scoped to one brand, 5-60 pax depending on client; (2) Shared — auxiliary cover for clients who don't need a full-time roster, cost-efficient turn-key.
narrative_brief.primary_audience: FMCG brand managers, trade-marketing leads, key-account managers, and country directors at multinational consumer-goods companies operating in Singapore who own the in-store execution KPIs (share-of-shelf, MSL compliance, planogram adherence, OOS, promo compliance).
value_props: Scale: 350+ FMCG manpower island-wide (the single largest FMCG merchandising bench in Singapore)
Retention: >95% (industry-leading; attrition is the silent killer of merchandising programmes — Elitez beats it)

Tech moat: EMR-Intel — proprietary field-force management SaaS. Mobile app for the field (multilingual EN/CN/BM), QuickView back-office for supervisors, BI dashboards for the brand (executive summary, weekly call achievement, MSL compliance, share-of-shelf, share-of-display, promo compliance, daily OOS trend, in-store activity, competitor price-check, photo gallery, attendance)
Proven roster: L'Oréal (cosmetics CPD+LUXE+PPD+ACD), Unilever, Shiseido, Pernod Ricard, Reckitt, FairPrice (house brand), Oatside, Suntory, Coty, Walch, Gardenia, Fresenius Kabi
Government-aligned: Elitez Group is a WSG Human Capital Partner, IHRP exclusive recruitment partner, SkillsFuture-approved training organization — credibility ladder visitors recognise
Backed by Elitez Group: 15 years, 311,960 payroll processed, 263,138 outsourced, 6,228 placements, 9 offices across SEA
differentiators_vs_competitors: EMR-Intel tech stack (most SG merch agencies are Excel + WhatsApp)
Both dedicated AND shared models under one roof (most agencies do one or the other)

Tier-1 logo roster including L'Oréal's full beauty portfolio (CPD: L'Oréal Paris, Garnier, Maybelline; LUXE: Lancôme, Kiehl's, Shu Uemura, Urban Decay, IT Cosmetics, YSL, Biotherm, Giorgio Armani; PPD: Kérastase, L'Oréal Pro; ACD: La Roche-Posay)
Continuous-improvement workflow (PowerBI integration, structured routing, KPI dashboards) vs ad-hoc deployments
Free replacement of underperforming merchandisers at no extra cost
narrative_brief.primary_cta: Book a 15-min discovery call with Jack Wang
industries_served: Food & Beverage (Pernod Ricard, Oatside, Suntory, Gardenia, Phoon Huat, F.East)
retail_channels_covered: Supermarkets (FairPrice, Cold Storage, Giant, Sheng Siong)
Specialty / department (Sasa, Sephora-channel beauty counters, Decathlon)

service_lines.name: Dedicated Merchandising
service_lines.summary: Full-time team scoped to one brand. Routing, KPIs, supervision, training, free replacement on under-performance. Team size 5-60 pax. Recommended for brands with sustained in-store presence.

service_lines.name: Shared Merchandising
service_lines.summary: Up-to-6-pax shared bench providing ad-hoc coverage of crucial outlets across supermarkets, pharmacies and retail. Available almost immediately. Cost-efficient for brands without full-time roster needs.

service_lines.name: EMR-Intel — Field Force Management
service_lines.summary: Proprietary enterprise SaaS bundled with merchandising contracts. Mobile app (EN/CN/BM): attendance + GPS check-in, journey planning, planogram, promotion compliance, stock check, price check, secondary display, share-of-shelf, share-of-space, competitor report, photo upload, eContent SOP reference, feedback workflow. QuickView back-office for supervisors. BI dashboards for brand HQ.

service_lines.name: Trade-Marketing Execution
service_lines.summary: POSM deployment, space-buy execution, marketing-promotion run-out, secondary display setup, NPI launch coverage, team bonding, training & development.

service_lines.name: Merchandising Audit
service_lines.summary: Third-party audits of in-store compliance — workflow tracking (modelled on 2024/25 L'Oréal Merchandising & Audit programme), structured channel coverage with clear lead-time reporting.

narrative_brief.tone_register: restrained, institutional, B2B, government-adjacent (BCA / WSG / PDPC register). No emojis. No marketing-speak. No exclamation marks in body copy. Cite numbers where credible. Operational concreteness beats brand poetry — Elitez sells reliability.
